sensoryProfile.sight: The canopy thins into defended ground, pale ward monoliths, old standing stones, road verges marked at intervals, and then open farmland once the last ward-stone falls behind.
sensoryProfile.sound: Pass wind, ward vibration, gravel and hooves on rough road, then the sudden absence of the ward-hum once the party crosses fully south.
sensoryProfile.smell: Mountain air, worked stone, dust, wet ground, and then harsher field air carrying pens, trough water, and open-country cold.
sensoryProfile.touch: Sharper wind, rougher road, a bass note from the wards felt in the teeth, and a hard magical impact when the damping effect ends.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: A true threshold: home ground while the wards hold, harsher and more exposed the instant they do not.

establishedFacts.fact: The approach south grows rougher and more exposed as the land slopes toward the pass.
The ward network is physically stronger and more noticeable near the pass, affecting both riders and mounts.
Scouts, smugglers, and raiders make the pass a place where Sylvanas goes exact and expects ambush geometry.
The ward network damps the southbound contamination enough that Jaina feels the full force only after the party leaves the protected line.
Crossing out of Quel'Thalas feels immediate in body terms: the managed warmth falls away, the escort formation tightens, and the road turns from curated border to vulnerable countryside.

eventsHere.event: Sylvanas rides hard south while revising the threat from paperwork nuisance into a genuine border emergency.
The escort leaves the pass with Jaina restored to her staff, and the full contamination field hits once the last ward-stone is behind them.
